name: Spanish
register: A reverent contemporary register in the Reina-Valera family (RVR1960) that Spanish-speaking Christians across Latin America and Spain know and love, softened toward the warmth and accessibility of the Nueva Versión Internacional (NVI) — dignified and devotional, using the familiar 'Padre' of the Padrenuestro rather than archaic forms.
quote_convention: Angular quotation marks «…» (comillas latinas) for direct speech, nesting with "…"; the dialogue dash/raya (—) is not used for Scripture speech.
poetry_convention: Poetry set as broken verse lines: each poetic stich on its own line, secondary parallel lines further indented, stanzas separated by a blank line — as RVR/NVI format the Psalms and the Prophets.

glossary.note: Filial divine name; 'celestial' modifies the warm 'Padre' (cf. RVR 'vuestro Padre celestial', Mt 6:14) — never 'nuestro Dios'.
Primary filial divine name — the 'Padre' of the Padrenuestro (Mt 6:9), the beloved-child address; NEVER revert to Dios or Señor.
Definite filial form (cf. Jn 5:19); the divine Father as person, not the generic 'Dios'.
Filial form without possessive; standard RVR/NVI collocation, retains 'Padre' not 'Dios'.
Aramaic address retained and glossed exactly as the most-loved text does (Ro 8:15; Mr 14:36; Gá 4:6) — the child's cry, kept filial.
REVERSION / non-filial contrast term. Only where the English keeps God-as-God; must never stand in for 'nuestro Padre'.

REVERSION / non-filial contrast term — the covenant name in the most-loved RVR1960; NVI/LBLA render 'el SEÑOR' in small caps. Distinct from 'Señor' (Lord, title for Jesus). Never a substitute for 'nuestro Padre'.
Fixed term across the whole Spanish Bible tradition.
Relational genitive, not by-identity; matches RVR 'el Espíritu de vuestro Padre' (Mt 10:20) with the filial 'Padre'.
Fixed christological title; standard RVR/NVI wording.
The Son (Jesus) in filial relation to el Padre.
Fixed christological title; 'Dios' is retained here because the title is the confession 'Son of God', contrasting the filial 'Padre'. Reviewer to confirm vs 'el Hijo del Padre'.
Jn 3:16; RVR1960 has the classic 'Hijo unigénito' (only-begotten), 'único' is the warm contemporary form (NVI) — reviewer's call on which register.
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially; precedent in 'el reino de mi Padre' (Mt 26:29) — keeps 'Padre', not 'de Dios'.
Fixed.
Fixed.
Standard transliteration; used where the English keeps the Hebrew title (cf. Jn 1:41).
Standard evangelical term (RVR/LBLA); Catholic tradition prefers 'alianza'.
Renders hesed (loyal covenant love); NVI often 'gran amor', RVR 'misericordia'. Reviewer to confirm the loyal-love force is felt vs a plain 'gran amor'.
Renders racham / splanchnizomai; for the gut-level tenderness the RVR 'entrañable misericordia' (heartfelt mercy, lit. 'from the bowels') is available if the context needs the visceral note.
Standard term; Spanish 'justicia' carries both righteousness and justice, as in RVR/NVI.
Fixed rendering (Jn 3:16) across the tradition.
Jn 3:3,7; the settled RVR/NVI phrase — carries the 'again/from above' sense the passage plays on.
